response:
Sec. 1  
people allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies cannot process the chemical compound peanut allergen which is present in peanuts  
the chemical compound peanut allergen is found in the flesh of peanuts and is responsible for triggering allergic reactions in sensitive individuals  
allergic reactions involve the immune system responding to the peanut allergen as a foreign substance  
the immune system releases chemicals that cause symptoms such as hives and swelling  
the body's response to peanut allergen is not compatible with the environment underwater  
the environment underwater is different from land and does not contain the peanut allergen  
the absence of peanut allergen in underwater environment means no allergic reactions  
the body's ability to process allergens is not affected by the underwater environment  
the physical properties of water do not influence the body's ability to handle allergens  
the body's immune system is not affected by the physical properties of water
